title: Expand OpenAI partnership
decisionQuestion: Should Microsoft deepen its multiyear investment in OpenAI's supercomputing and research?
situation: Microsoft is announcing the third phase of its OpenAI partnership, following 2019 and 2021 investments, to accelerate AI breakthroughs.
objectives: Deploy OpenAI models across consumer and enterprise products
Establish Azure as OpenAI's exclusive cloud provider
conditions: OpenAI must independently commercialize resulting technologies
Azure powers all OpenAI workloads across research, products, and API services
constraints: Investment must target substantial opportunity and growth areas

options.description: Commit to a multiyear, multibillion dollar investment in OpenAI supercomputing and research
options.expectedBenefit: Accelerates AI breakthroughs and secures Azure as OpenAI's exclusive cloud provider
options.principalRisk: Large capital commitment to a single partner's research direction

options.description: Continue existing collaboration without new multibillion dollar investment
options.expectedBenefit: Avoids large capital outlay and maintains flexibility
options.principalRisk: Loses exclusive cloud position and falls behind in AI model deployment

rejectedBenefit: Avoids large capital outlay and maintains flexibility
observations: This is the third phase of a partnership that began in 2019
Azure will power all OpenAI workloads across research, products, and API services
Amy Hood stated the importance of investing where there is substantial opportunity and growth
inferences: The exclusive cloud provider status creates a long-term lock-in for Azure
The multibillion dollar scale signals Microsoft's view of AI as a generational technology
Independent commercialization clauses protect both companies from antitrust and competition concerns

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: Today, we are announcing the third phase of our long-term partnership with OpenAI through a multiyear, multibillion dollar investment to accelerate AI breakthroughs to ensure these benefits are broadly shared with the world.

This agreement follows our previous investments in 2019 and 2021. It extends our ongoing collaboration across AI supercomputing and research and enables each of us to independently commercialize the resulting advanced AI technologies.

Supercomputing at scale – Microsoft will increase our investments in the development and deployment of specialized supercomputing systems to accelerate OpenAI’s groundbreaking independent AI research.
New AI-powered experiences – Microsoft will deploy OpenAI’s models across our consumer and enterprise products and introduce new categories of digital experiences built on OpenAI’s technology.
Exclusive cloud provider – As OpenAI’s exclusive cloud provider, Azure will power all OpenAI workloads across research, products and API services.
I think it’s important in terms of how we think about investment and continuing to invest where we’re seeing substantial opportunity and growth.
evidenceSpans.speaker: Amy Hood
